items.title: The Overlooked Power of Frontend Performance Optimization
items.author: Nasir Movlamov
items.content:
Most frontend developers focus on building beautiful, interactive user interfaces. But how often do we prioritize performance with the same level of attention?

Frontend performance is not just about loading times; it's about user experience, conversion rates, and even SEO rankings. A sluggish interface can cost a business customers, no matter how well-designed it is.

Here are some optimization strategies that are often overlooked:

Efficient State Management – Unnecessary re-renders in React applications can significantly slow down performance. Techniques like memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback) and optimizing context usage can make a big difference.

Minimizing Third-Party Dependencies – Every additional library adds to the bundle size. Before installing a package, consider if it can be replaced with native browser APIs or a lighter alternative.

Optimized Rendering with Concurrent Features – React’s Concurrent Mode and Suspense help avoid blocking the main thread, improving responsiveness. Understanding how to use these effectively is crucial for modern applications.

Adaptive Loading Strategies – Not all users have high-speed connections. Implementing lazy loading, code splitting, and serving different assets based on network conditions can enhance accessibility and performance.

Avoiding Unnecessary DOM Manipulations – Overuse of direct DOM manipulation (even via libraries like jQuery) can cause performance bottlenecks. Leveraging React’s virtual DOM properly prevents unnecessary updates.

But here’s something often ignored: network constraints matter more than you think.

A 1MB JavaScript bundle takes approximately:

  • 30+ seconds on 2G (essentially unusable)
  • 8–10 seconds on 3G (frustrating for users)
  • 1.5–3 seconds on 4G (still noticeable)

Here’s the average internet speeds for these network types:

    Now imagine your app loads 2MB+ of JavaScript before becoming interactive. That’s an instant deal-breaker for users in areas with unstable connections.

    Let’s look at some common package sizes:

    • Lodash – ~72KB minified & gzipped (often used unnecessarily)
    • Moment.js – ~67KB (better alternatives: date-fns, Luxon)
    • Ant Design – ~1MB (heavy UI library; consider tree-shaking or alternatives like Radix UI)
    • React PDF – ~500KB (great for PDFs, but heavy for simple cases)
    • jQuery – ~90KB (do you really need this in 2025?)

    If your project includes all these dependencies without optimization, you could easily ship over 2MB of JavaScript before the user interacts with the page. That means:

    • 40+ seconds on 2G (practically unusable)
    • 12–16 seconds on 3G (most users will bounce)
    • 4–6 seconds on 4G (noticeable delay, impacting UX and SEO)
    • This is why performance optimization isn’t optional—it’s a necessity.

    Some ways to mitigate this: ✔️ Replace large libraries with lighter alternatives (e.g., use date-fns ✔️ Use tree-shaking to remove unused code from heavy UI libraries

    ✔️ Implement lazy loading for components and assets

    ✔️ Split bundles using dynamic imports to only load what’s needed

    Optimizing frontend performance is not just a technical challenge; it's a user experience imperative. In an era where milliseconds matter, a well-optimized fron-tend can be the key differentiator for digital products.

items.title: Secure Authentication with JWTs & Rotating Refresh Tokens (TypeScript + Express + Vanilla JS)
items.author: Wiljeder Filho
items.content:
Authentication is a fundamental part of web applications, and handling it securely is crucial. Many developers store both accessToken and refreshToken in localStorage, but this exposes the application to XSS attacks.

A better approach is: ✅ Store the refreshToken in an HTTP-only cookie (prevents XSS)

✅ Store the accessToken in localStorage or memory (easy access for API calls)

✅ Use rotating refresh tokens (each refresh invalidates the previous one)

In this guide, we'll build a simple authentication system using Express (TypeScript) for the backend and Vanilla JavaScript for the frontend.

Overview of JWT Authentication Flow

Before diving into code, let's understand the authentication flow:

  1. : The user provides credentials, and the server verifies them.
  2. : Upon successful authentication, the server issues two tokens:
    • A short-lived access token (e.g., 15 minutes)
    • A long-lived refresh token (e.g., 7 days)
  3. :
    • Access token is stored in localStorage or memory
    • Refresh token is stored in an HTTP-only cookie
  4. : The client includes the access token in the Authorization header for API requests
  5. : When the access token expires, the client uses the refresh token to get a new access token
  6. : Each refresh invalidates the previous refresh token, enhancing security

We begin by importing the necessary packages, configuring our app to use JSON, cookies, and CORS, and initializing environment variables with dotenv:

Notice the and specifying the frontend array ensure that cookies are sent with requests to our server from those domains.

For simplicity, we’ll store our user and token data in memory. In production, replace this with a real database (like PostgreSQL, MongoDB, etc.):

  • holds each user’s valid refresh token. If a token isn’t in here, it’s invalid.
  • We mark everything as () just for clarity in our toy example.

Secrets & Utility Functions

We read our JWT secrets from environment variables, then define two helper functions to generate tokens:

  • ():
    • Short expiration (5 seconds here just for demonstration).
    • Should contain minimal user info (here, only ).
  • ():
    • Longer expiration (7 days).
    • Includes a to facilitate more advanced revocation strategies if needed.
    • Stored in so only the latest token is valid.

When a user logs in, we generate an access token and a refresh token. The access token is returned in the JSON response, and the refresh token is stored in an HTTP-only cookie:

  • : If username/password don’t match, we return a 401.
  • : We get both the short-lived access and long-lived refresh tokens.
  • : means JavaScript can’t read this cookie, which protects against XSS.
  • : The client (frontend) will store the access token in (or in-memory) for easy usage in API calls.

5. Refresh Route (Token Rotation)

When the short-lived access token expires, the frontend sends a request to to get a new one. This route:

  1. Reads the old refresh token from the cookie.
  2. Validates it with .
  3. Ensures it matches the latest stored token for the user (prevents using old tokens).
  4. Generates brand-new tokens and invalidates the old refresh token, implementing rotation.
  • : By deleting the old token from db.refreshTokens and generating a new one each time, we ensure that once the old token has been used, it can’t be reused.
  • : If the token is invalid, we clear the refresh cookie and respond with 401 Unauthorized.

6. Auth Middleware (Protecting Routes)

This middleware verifies that the access token on the Authorization: Bearer ... header is valid. If it is, we attach the user info to ; if not, we return or .

  • We look for Authorization: Bearer .
  • If the token is expired or invalid, jwt.verify() triggers an error.
  • If everything checks out, the request proceeds to the next middleware/handler.

This is an example route that requires a valid access token. It won’t be reached unless our successfully verifies the token.

Finally, we just start our app listening on port 5000.

We have a simple HTML page and a corresponding JavaScript file. The HTML provides a basic login form and buttons for secret data fetching and logging out. The JS handles authentication logic (login, token refresh, and protected resource access).

Loading...LoginLoginGet Secret
        Logout

The JavaScript handles the authentication logic:

Key aspects of the frontend implementation:

  1. Access token is stored in localStorage
  2. Refresh token is handled automatically via HTTP-only cookies
  3. Automatic token refresh when making API calls
  4. Proper credentials inclusion for CORS requests with cookies

This implementation provides several security advantages:

1. Protection Against XSS Attacks

By storing the refresh token in an HTTP-only cookie, we protect it from JavaScript access. Even if an attacker manages to inject malicious JavaScript, they cannot steal the refresh token.

Each time a refresh token is used, a new refresh token is issued and the old one is invalidated. This means that if a refresh token is somehow compromised, it becomes useless as soon as it's used.

3. Short-lived Access Tokens

By keeping access tokens short-lived (5-15 minutes in production), we minimize the window of opportunity for attackers if an access token is somehow leaked.

Practical Considerations for Production

When implementing this in a production environment:

  1. : Adjust the access token expiration to a practical value (5-15 minutes)
  2. : Use a persistent database to store refresh tokens
  3. : Implement a mechanism to clean up expired refresh tokens
  4. : Add rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks
  5. : Ensure all communication happens over HTTPS
  6. : Use proper password hashing (bcrypt/Argon2) instead of storing plaintext passwords
  7. : Implement account lockout after multiple failed attempts

This tutorial demonstrates a secure approach to JWT authentication using Express and vanilla JavaScript. By storing refresh tokens in HTTP-only cookies and implementing token rotation, we significantly enhance the security of our authentication system while maintaining a seamless user experience.

The complete source code is available on this GitHub repo, where you can see the full implementation in action.

items.title: Top 10 Voice Call APIs in 2025
items.author: Rajeshwari Kalyanasundaram
items.content:
"Find here the top voice call SDKs in the market that help you build a crystal-clear communication app and foster seamless conversation."

Want to build a communication app like WhatsApp or FB so that your users can engage in one-to-one audio calls or group conference calls? Then you must use a voice call SDK. Because, with no or minimal codes, you can integrate all the advanced calling capabilities in minutes.

But which to opt for? That is what this blog is all about, helping you discover the best 10 phone call SDK providers in town that developers love to create their own voice chat app. And before we delve into this topic, let us see its basics.

What Is A Voice Call API?

A voice call API (Application Programming Interface) is a technology that allows developers to easily add advanced voice calling features like single and group calls, call recording, file sharing, and other attributes into their apps, communication systems like VoIP, or websites without writing codes from scratch or ground zero.

And by now, these voice call SDKs are highly used by businesses to create solutions like call center software, support systems, click-to-call apps, and many more.

Best 10 Voice Call API Provider For Lasting Engagements

There are plenty of providers in the market that excel in features, pricing, and customizability. Hence, we have come up with the top 10 API solutions that are bound to offer the best services, user-friendliness, and pricing.

MirrorFly comes to businesses and developers as one of the leading CPaaS providers of in-app video, voice, and chat solutions, helping them to build top-notch, powerful, and engaging communication apps.

Besides the fact that the provider offers 1000+ call and chat features like single and group calls, call muting, call logs, and others, their prime highlight is the self-hosted solution. Meaning, with it, you can avail complete customizations on their source code and build a chat software that your business would want to.

Along with this, their APIs and SDKs offer great scalability, tight security, multi-tenancy support, pre-built UI kits, and much more to build engaging voice calling apps.

  1. The platform's APIs and SDKs can easily handle the volume of any traffic.
  2. Supports integration with other tools and software
  3. Offers 100% customization on their source code and offers whitelabeling.
  4. Does not require any coding knowledge and you can even hire developers to complete setup.
  1. Features are abundant, and need thorough understanding of them.
  2. You must contact sales for detailed pricing.

Twilio is one of the leading in-app voice calling API providers in town that offers low-latency voice communication along with messaging and video calling capabilities too.

Besides these, the platform also supports integrating some of the most-searched voice communication features like call routing, recording, transcription, and SMS too. Also, Twilio stands as the top choice for developers because of its reliability, scalability, and detailed guides.

  1. It offers customization options on its features.
  2. Twilio supports integration with third-party apps.
  1. Pricing is high when compared to other providers.
  2. You need expertise in coding as the setup is difficult.

Apphitect comes to developers as one of the best and most reliable whitelabel communication SDK providers that offers 100% customization on their video and voice calling, and messaging capabilities. Plus, they provide self-hosted solutions to businesses so that they give flexibility to them to host their data and apps on their own servers and take full ownership.

Also, their APIs are designed to be secure, scalable, and render 99.999% uptime SLA. All of which are great to deliver perfect messaging experiences.

  1. Presence of abundant voice calling features that enhances user engagement.
  2. Supports integration with CRM and other third-party software.
  1. One needs technical knowledge to use their APIs and SDK.

PubNub, is one of the unique voice API providers that comes as a global data stream network to businesses who specialize in offering real-time communication solutions for IoT and mobile apps. Plus, it offers low-latency calling and is designed for chat applications that require high concurrency.

Also it has some of the unique features like data synchronization, presence detection, live collaboration, and push notification.

1.Easy to integrate into apps.

  1. Supports live data streaming too along with voice calling.
  1. PubNub caters to limited use cases.

The next provider in the list is Plivo that is known for its robust and powerful APIs and SDKs that caters to businesses of all kinds. It offers attributes like call routing, IVRs, and voice broadcasting.

Along with this, it also helps developers to add automated workflows, voice notifications, and many more advanced features. They also offer know-how guides to educate businesses on the implementation capabilities.

  1. Plivo offers PHLO which is a no-code builder for non-coders.
  2. It has special features to protect sensitive information.
  1. They do not have a customer support system to resolve queries.

With the help of Telnyx cloud communication platform, businesses can build scalable chat software with reliable features that users would simply love.

Plus, their voice API and SDK helps in integrating features like SIP trunking, call control, and call recording thus making it ideal for call center solutions. Also they come up with developer guides to train them on how to integrate their APIs.

  1. They have a very intuitive user-interface that makes it easy for quick navigation.
  2. Gives updates on a regular basis.
  1. Sometimes the users have faced issues related to security and transparency aspects.

Now this voice API provider helps businesses to manage their internal and external communication by adding features like IVR, call routing, messaging, and automated voice alerts.

Additionally, Exotel comes at a very low infrastructure cost with high-end security and compliance parameters thus making it suitable for e-commerce and telehealth industries.

  1. Developers can set up and use their service without any difficulties.
  2. The platform helps boost customer satisfaction rates through IVRs.
  1. Some users felt the interface to be outdated and less informative.

MessageBird is one of the phone call API providers that gives developers the flexibility to add inbound call automation capability along with analytics and voice messages. Also, it provides omnichannel support thus making it easy for businesses who need to add SMS, email, and WhatsApp into a single app.

Additionally, its AI-driven voice features help to build smart communication systems to deliver personalized experiences.

  1. Supports integration with other channels.
  2. Gives documentation to developers to guide them on the integration process.
  1. Set up codes are pricey and hence not suitable for small businesses.

From adding voice calling to conferencing to call management features, RingCentra's voice APIs help developers to build an amazing communication software that users would simply love. Plus, it supports VoIP calls at the best quality, call routing attribute, and CRM integration capability that makes it easy for enterprises who are looking to streamline their chat application.

Another merit of using this provider is that they give the flexibility to integrate with tools like Teams, Salesforce, and Slack to boost customer engagement levels.

  1. Offers advanced call management features that most of the other peers do not.
  2. A single platform combines video, voice, and chat features.
  1. Pricing is very high compared to other providers.
  2. Certain features need a learning curve thus making it difficult for newbie developers.

The last one in our list is Sinch that is fastly growing as one of the reliable providers of voice APIs and helping companies build high-quality apps with advanced features like SIP trunking, call verification, programmable calling, and more.

Plus, this cloud communication platform is easy to use, secure, scalable, and has an AI-powered voice bot support which makes it easy to build customer support applications.

  1. Offers great quality voice calls.
  2. Supports different programming languages and frameworks.
  1. Pricing is a bit difficult to understand and platform is a little complex

Key Features To Look For In A Voice Call API

Now that you are satisfied with the thought that audio calling SDKs and APIs are highly beneficial for your business, when you choose one, it is required that you see for the following factors:

  1. Customization options: Always go ahead with third-party audio call SDK providers who give you endless customization capability so that you can tailor create your app based on your business needs.

  2. Scalability: You want an API that can grow when your business grows so that your app can easily handle call volumes.

  3. Security: This is one of the crucial factors as you do not need your data to be hampered by prying eyes. So look for providers who promise E2E encryption and are compliant with industry regulatory standards.

  4. Abundant features: Your customers or users would be glued to your app only if they find it to be impressive and to make one, you need features like call recording, single chats, conferencing, text-to-speech, call routing, scheduling, transferring, and much more.

  5. Low latency: Ensure that your choice of provider offers your users to make high-quality calls without any glitches or hitches.

  6. Compatibility: Look out for voice call APIs that work seamlessly well across different platforms like web, mobile, and VoIP.

With technology advancing at a much faster pace, it has become easy for businesses to build instant voice-enabled apps using an audio call API and SDK.

But, they lack guidance in which provider to choose for their business needs and that's where we stepped up and gave you a detailed insight on each of the top providers in town.

So, do take a minute to go through each before making a decision and yes happy integrating!

items.title: The Power of Authenticity: Why Transparency is the Key to a Strong Personal Brand
items.author: Okoye Ndidiamaka
items.content:
Ever felt like some personal brands are too perfect? You scroll through social media and catch glimpses of influencers, entrepreneurs, and thought leaders posting perfect success stories. No setbacks, no failures—just a highlight reel of victories. But deep down, something doesn't feel right. It's inspiring, yes, but is it relatable?

Here's the truth: People don't connect with perfection. They connect with honesty.

Now, in our contemporary age of digital niceties, where authenticity is currency, honesty and integrity are the greatest assets of your personal brand. If you're going to differentiate yourself, be believable, and leave a lasting impression, you need to be genuine.

So how do you build a personal brand that people believe in? Let's get started.

  1. Share Your Journey—Not Just the Highlights Imagine following someone who only talks about their success—how they built their business, acquired perfect clients, or established a massive following. It's great, but context-less, it's distant.

What human beings really need is the origin story of the success.

✅ Talk about what you overcame. ✅ Talk about what you learned.

✅ Show how you persisted.

Authenticity isn't about divulging too much personal stuff; it's about exposing the process of the progress.

Tip: Instead of merely stating, "I built a six-figure company," share how you started from zero followers, were rejected, and struggled with imposter syndrome before getting it all together.

👉 Action Tip: The next time you post, ask yourself: Am I only posting the result, or am I sharing the journey?

  1. Be Clear About Your Values Your personal brand has to mean something. People relate to people who understand what they believe in, what they stand for, and what they value.

Ask yourself: ✔️ What do I believe in?

✔️ What values do I want others to see me for?

✔️ How can I be consistent in expressing those values?

Transparency is not what you do—it's why you do it.

Tip: If you're an AI ethicist coder, talk about using technology responsibly. If you're a sustainable fashion influencer, talk about the brands you support.

👉Action Tip: Create content that's authentic to what you believe in, so your readers know what you care about.

  1. Act Like a Person, Not a Brand Ever sent someone a message or comment, then been ignored or met with a robot reply? Doesn't feel very personal, does it?

Real engagement encourages trust. Want your audience to see you as a real brand? You've got to write like a living human.

✅ Reply thoughtfully to comments. ✅ Reply to what your audience are saying.

✅ Start some real conversations.

Tip: Instead of just commenting "Thanks for your comment," try commenting something like "That's a great point! What's your opinion on this?" to get the conversation going.

👉 Action Tip: Spend 10-15 minutes a day engaging with your fans—posting is not enough, but responding to their comments and feedback.

  1. Share Behind-the-Scenes Moments Everyone is curious about what happens behind closed doors. It makes your brand more authentic.

✅ Share your workspace setup. ✅ Show the brainstorming process behind your ideas.

✅ Give a glimpse of work-in-progress projects.

Behind-the-scenes content helps humanize your brand and makes people feel connected to you.

💡 Example: If you’re launching a product or service, share sneak peeks, struggles, and the creative process instead of waiting until everything is perfect.

👉 Action Tip: Post one behind-the-scenes update this week and see how your audience reacts!

  1. Stay Consistent—Authenticity is a Long-Term Game Authenticity isn't a temporary strategy—it's a continuous process. You need to continue showing up, creating value, and being authentic.

✔️ Stop trying to be part of the in-crowd with a fabricated image. ✔️ Stop pretending to be an expert when you're still learning.

✔️ Stop changing your core message to follow trends.

Human beings believe in consistency. When your brand messaging keeps shifting, your audience will have no clue what to expect next.

💡 Example: If you’re building a brand around tech innovation, stick to topics that align with that theme instead of jumping to unrelated trends just for views.

👉 Action Tip: Do a quick audit of your recent posts—are they aligned with your brand values and message?

Final Thoughts: Your Authenticity is Your Superpower In a world where social media often feels curated, authenticity stands out.

By being authentic, interactive, and truthful, you don't just build a personal brand—you build trust, credibility, and meaningful relationships.

How do you bring authenticity to your brand?

Have you ever been following someone because they came across as authentic?

Share your thoughts in the comments! Let's talk. ⬇️

items.title: 100 Compliments for Your Partner to Sweeten Your Everyday Life!
items.author: james lin
items.content:
Have You Ever Upset Your Girlfriend Without Realizing It?

I recently found myself in trouble with my girlfriend again. She excitedly showed me her new dress and asked, "Do I look good?" I simply replied, "You look great!" But instead of a happy reaction, she got upset. I was completely confused. How could saying she looks good be wrong? While racking my brain for a way to make things right, I had a sudden realization—why not seek help from AI? After all, AI can assist with almost anything nowadays!

AI to the Rescue: Learning the Art of Compliments

I opened XXAI, the ultimate AI assistant, and chose GPT-4o to analyze my situation. Within seconds, it gave me an insightful answer, and suddenly, everything made sense—I needed to level up my emotional intelligence. Looking back, I realized there had been many times when I didn't understand why my girlfriend was upset. Sometimes, I even thought she was being unreasonable. But AI’s analysis helped me see things differently.

Why Compliments Matter in a Relationship

In relationships, it’s crucial to pay attention, empathize, and communicate genuinely. Personalized compliments can make your partner feel truly cherished. Ironically, we often take those closest to us for granted. AI-powered relationship coaching isn’t just about solving arguments—it’s about helping us build stronger emotional connections. A Simple Fix: A Little Effort Goes a Long Way

Taking AI’s advice, I decided to surprise my girlfriend with a bouquet of flowers. Along with the flowers, I used the magic words AI suggested:

"Baby, I was wrong—completely wrong. How could I just say ‘good’ when the most beautiful person in this dress is my girlfriend? Not praising you properly is a crime I’d be punished for in my next life!"

Her face instantly lit up with a smile. "Wow, what’s gotten into you today? You’re never this sweet! Did you get help from some kind of love guru?"

I secretly laughed—she had no idea my ‘love guru’ was AI!

Why AI is a Relationship Game-Changer

Have you ever struggled with similar situations in your relationship? AI-driven emotional intelligence tools like XXAI can help. But maybe the issue isn’t that we lack emotional intelligence—it’s that we don’t always pay enough attention. We get so caught up in work and life that we forget to cherish those closest to us. AI isn’t just a crisis manager for when things go wrong—it’s a relationship coach that teaches us how to be more thoughtful every day. Small gestures and the right words can make a big difference.

A Happier, Sweeter Life with AI

Thanks to AI, I’ve learned how to express my feelings better and make my relationship even sweeter. If AI can help me, it can help you too! Whether it’s finding the right words or understanding your partner’s emotions, AI is the ultimate modern-day romance assistant.

items.title: WeakMap in react-scan vs next-mdx source code.
items.author: Ramu Narasinga
items.content:
const cache = new WeakMap<object, string>();

export function fastSerialize(value: unknown, depth = 0): string {
  ...
  if (value === null) return 'null';

  if (cache.has(value)) {
    const cached = cache.get(value);
    if (cached !== undefined) {
      return cached;
    }
  }

This is the second time I am writing about WeakMap. Previously, I wrote an article about WeakMap in JavaScript and this was a review about a code snippet from next-mdx source code and the code looks like below:

const cache = new WeakMap()

/**
 * A webpack loader for mdx-rs. This is largely based on existing @mdx-js/loader,
 * replaces internal compilation logic to use mdx-rs instead.
 */
function loader(value, bindings, callback) {
  ...
  const compiler = this._compiler || marker

  let map = cache.get(compiler)

  if (!map) {
    map = new Map()
    cache.set(compiler, map)
  }
...

What’s one thing that’s common here? cache….

Below is how cache is initialized in react-scan source code:

const cache = new WeakMap<object, string>();

Below is how cache is initialized in next-mdx source code

const cache = new WeakMap()

Well, there is types in the react-scan source code.

A is a collection of key/value pairs whose keys must be objects or non-registered symbols, with values of any arbitrary JavaScript type, and which does not create strong references to its keys. That is, an object's presence as a key in a does not prevent the object from being garbage collected. Once an object used as a key has been collected, its corresponding values in any become candidates for garbage collection as well — as long as they aren't strongly referred to elsewhere. The only primitive type that can be used as a key is symbol — more specifically, non-registered symbols — because non-registered symbols are guaranteed to be unique and cannot be re-created.

Hey, my name is Ramu Narasinga. I study large open-source projects and create content about their codebase architecture and best practices, sharing it through articles, videos.

items.title: MiniScript's Parent-Class Variable Pitfall
items.author: BibleClinger
items.content:
Let's say you're wanting to use MiniScript to write a game for Mini Micro. You're making a Shoot'em Up (or SHMUP for short). This is an older genre of video game that used to be quite popular. Let's say you want to make a bunch of enemy fighters for the player to shoot at in rapid succession as they try to shoot back at the player.

Now let's suppose you have a background in an object oriented programming language like Java. For your enemy fighters, you figure you should make a class like this:

We are defining two properties for an enemy fighter:

  1. The shot strength: how many damage units their shot does to the player ship. By default we want 1 unit of damage.
  2. The cargo that the enemy fighter is carrying. This will translate to what items will drop when the enemy ship is destroyed. This is how the player will pick up loot.

To simplify the loot system, let's just give a list of integers, with each integer corresponding to an item in some lookup table. Let's spawn three enemies:

We are intending the following:

  • The first ship has a shot strength of 1 and a single cargo item of id 0.
  • The 2nd enemy fighter has shot strength 2 and a single cargo item of id 1.
  • The 3rd and last enemy has a shot strength of 3 and a single cargo item of id 2.

Now adding bullets, collision detection, animations, and the destruction of the enemy fighter are all necessary, but let's bypass that and just test if our code is correct this far. First we'll write a function for to print its internal variables when destroyed.

Now let's add some debugging to see what happens when the ship is destroyed.

Enemy Fighter destroyed: 1; [0, 1, 2]
Enemy Fighter destroyed: 2; [0, 1, 2]
Enemy Fighter destroyed: 3; [0, 1, 2]

Oh, that's not good. All three enemy fighters are showing that they are carrying three cargo items!

At first glance it might look like we made a mistake and added all of the cargo items to each fighter explicitly in our loop, but we didn't; we added one item per fighter. The actual bug is harder to spot. Can you find it?

Let's take a peek at a representation of the class and the list:

There's only one instance of and it belongs to . In Java terms, is behaving more like a static class variable than an instance variable. Wow. How did that happen?

MiniScript uses Prototype Inheritance for its implementation of Object Oriented Programming. There is no difference between classes and objects. In fact, both classes and objects are just Maps.

When you use the keyword, you are making a new map with one entry: . This entry is what MiniScript uses to go up the inheritance chain.

But why did MiniScript duplicate for every instance object? Because we explicitly set it in the child instances. MiniScript adds shadow variables to these instances when we set a variable so we don't have this issue.

But why didn't it duplicate for us? Because we didn't explicitly set the value of for each child instance. Instead we merely pushed another element into the already existing list. Where is that existing list? Inside .

There are multiple ways of fixing this. Here's the simple way to fix this example: explicitly add the line directly before you push the cargo index value. This will create a separate cargo list for each enemy fighter.

As an aside, something that might be hard to wrap one's brain around is the fact that the class could actually be entirely empty of properties, since we aren't using any functionality on this object directly.

Remember is just a map. It's not inherently an object or a class -- it can be used however we want it to be. The instances of are also just maps. The problem is simple: if we are editing a list that is actually found in the parent instance, then naturally this will be reflected in all child instances. They are all using the same list, after all.

The Factory Method Pattern

We don't have constructors in MiniScript. Instead, we can make our own factory function that produces objects. Let's rewrite the EnemyFighter class entirely. We want the following features:

  1. instance creation is handled by a member function that is similar to a static method on a class.
  2. All instances of are stored in a list found within the class itself.
  3. removes its instance from this internal list.

Above is the secret internals of the implementation. If you are writing a sort of game engine for your team to use, they hopefully don't need to know too much about the above code. Rather, they just need to know how to use it.

Now our enemy creation loop might look like this:

Cleaner! And our debug code to simulate destroying all the enemies could be this:

Enemy Fighter destroyed: 1; [0]
Enemy Fighter destroyed: 2; [1]
Enemy Fighter destroyed: 3; [2]

MiniScript's implementation of the OOP paradigm can be a bit tricky. This is especially problematic if you experience something like the above while working hard on a 48 hour game jam where time is a commodity you can't afford to lose much of when tracking down bugs. We can mitigate the difficulties by writing clean instancing code, and adhering to certain programming patterns.

I'll leave you with this last thought: It might be argued that MiniScript's implementation of OOP is actually quite simple, but part of what adds complexity is the baggage we are bringing from other OOP languages.

If you agree or disagree, please let me know in the comments. Alternatively, if you have other ideas on object creation, suggestions for better patterns or practices, or related contemplations on MiniScript, also leave a comment.

items.title: Top Website Design Trends in 2025
items.author: DM intern 10
items.content:
1. Minimalist and Clean Designs Simple layouts with plenty of white space improve readability and user focus. Clean navigation enhances user experience and increases session time.
  2. Dark Mode and Customizable Themes Dark mode continues to dominate due to its sleek aesthetic and reduced eye strain. Offering users a choice between light and dark themes increases engagement.
  3. AI-Powered Personalization Websites are using AI to analyze user behavior and deliver personalized content. Dynamic product recommendations and tailored user journeys improve conversions.
  4. 3D Graphics and Immersive Elements Interactive 3D visuals and animations enhance user engagement. Parallax scrolling and depth effects create a more engaging experience.
  5. Micro interactions and Motion UI Subtle animations (like button hover effects) make the website feel more interactive. Motion UI enhances user feedback and guides user behavior.
  6. Mobile-First and Responsive Design With mobile usage dominating, websites must adapt seamlessly to different devices. Fast loading and touch-friendly elements improve user experience.
  7. Voice Search Optimization More users are using voice commands for search. Optimizing content for conversational search terms improves visibility.
  8. Sustainable and Eco-Friendly Design Reducing energy consumption through efficient coding and lighter graphics. Green hosting solutions help improve the brand's reputation. By adopting these website design trends in 2025, businesses can enhance user experience, improve search rankings, and increase conversions. Staying ahead of design trends ensures your website remains competitive and engaging in the ever-evolving digital landscape.

items.title: Master Data Structures and Algorithms - A Practical Approach
items.author: Ojas ATG
items.content:
As a college student aiming to excel in Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA), it's crucial to approach learning with a strategic plan rather than investing in expensive courses. Spending money on a DSA course might not be the best use of your resources, as it often focuses on memorization rather than developing problem-solving skills essential for acing interviews. Here’s a week-by-week guide to help you master DSA without breaking the bank:

Week 1 & 2: Building Foundations

  1. Familiarize Yourself with Platforms: Solve 20-30 easy problems on platforms like LeetCode or HackerRank. This step helps you understand the format and how to approach problems.
  2. : If you encounter a difficult problem, review the solution and attempt it again. This process trains your brain to think critically and approach problems systematically.

Week 3, 4, & 5: Deep Dive into Concepts

  1. Optimize Previous Solutions: Take the easy problems you solved in the first two weeks and try to optimize them. This practice helps you understand how to improve efficiency.
  2. : Focus on understanding different data structures (e.g., arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs) and how to implement them in your preferred programming language. Don’t rush into solving problems yet; just get familiar with the concepts.
  3. : Learn and memorize key techniques such as hashing, sliding window, Kadane’s algorithm, etc. These tools will be invaluable in solving complex problems.
  4. Identify Problem Patterns:
    • : If a problem involves finding the Kth optimal element, it might be a heap question.
    • : If it asks for the most optimal result, it’s likely a DP problem. Start with recursion and then memoize it.
    • : Problems involving choices often require recursion.
    • : Develop your own patterns for these data structures.

Week 6 & 7: Practice with Standard Sets

  1. : Start by coding the brute force solution for each problem.
  2. : Look for similarities between problems and identify the patterns involved.
  3. : Recall the problem types and apply the techniques you’ve learned (e.g., hashing, DP).
  4. : Use the tools in your arsenal to optimize your solutions.

Afterwards: Consolidating Knowledge

By following this plan, you will have acquired most of the tools and techniques needed to tackle DSA problems effectively. The goal is to think about problems rationally rather than memorizing solutions from sheets. This approach not only saves you money but also equips you with the skills required to excel in coding interviews.

For further assistance, explore the following resources to enhance your learning journey:

By adopting this structured approach, you can master Data Structures and Algorithms without relying on expensive courses, ensuring you're well-prepared for coding interviews and future challenges in the field.

If you found this article useful, like, comment, and share, or just buy me a coffee?

items.title: Automate Next.js Deployment to AWS EC2 using GitHub Actions
items.author: Dinh Hoang Duy
items.content:
Automating your Next.js deployment process can significantly streamline updates and reduce manual effort. This guide will walk through setting up GitHub Actions to automatically deploy your Next.js application to an AWS EC2 instance.

Refer to the previous post for setting up the AWS EC2 instance, installing Node.js, configuring Nginx, and optimizing with Cloudflare.

1. Setting Up GitHub Secrets

Before configuring GitHub Actions, store the following secrets in your GitHub repository:

  • : Public IP of your EC2 instance
  • : SSH username (e.g., )
  • : Private key for SSH authentication
  • : Folder name where the app is stored on EC2
  • : PM2 process name
  1. Go to your repository on GitHub.
  2. Navigate to > > .
  3. Click and add the required secrets.

2. Creating the GitHub Actions Workflow

Create a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file in your repository with the following content:

  • : The workflow runs whenever new code is pushed to the branch.
  • : Node.js 20 and required npm packages are installed.
  • : Next.js is built before deployment.
  • : The app is securely copied to the instance using SCP.
  • :
    • Installs necessary dependencies on the server.
    • Ensures PM2 is running and restarts the application.

4. Testing the Deployment

  1. Push a commit to branch: 
git add 
git commit 
git push origin main

  1. Monitor the workflow under > .

  2. Verify the deployment on your EC2 instance:

1. SSH Authentication Fails

  • Ensure your private key matches the one on EC2 ().
  • Set correct permissions for the key: .
  • Run to check if the process is running.
  • Manually restart: pm2 restart <your-app-name>.

3. Deployment Not Updating

  • Ensure old files are removed: rm -rf /home/your-user/your-app/* before uploading.

With GitHub Actions, deploying Next.js to AWS EC2 is automated, efficient, and scalable. This setup allows for seamless updates, ensuring minimal downtime. Next, we’ll explore adding a CI/CD pipeline with automated testing!

items.title: CORS Done Wrong? Say Hello to Security Risks!
items.author: Ella
items.content:
If you build web applications, you often need to fetch data from different domains — whether it’s pulling information from an external API or loading resources from another server. But if you’ve ever run into a CORS error, you know how frustrating it can be!

When it happens, many developers simply modify server settings to “make CORS work,” but misconfiguring CORS can introduce security risks. In this article, we’ll explore how to properly configure CORS to enhance web security rather than weaken it.

CORS vs. Same-Origin Policy (SOP)

Before diving into CORS, let’s briefly discuss the —a security feature that prevents web pages from making requests to a different origin (domain, protocol, or port).

What is the Same-Origin Policy?

SOP ensures that scripts running on one website cannot access resources from another site unless explicitly allowed. This prevents malicious websites from making unauthorized API requests on behalf of users, which helps protect against unauthorized access to resources.

For example: ✅ A page hosted at access if CORS allows it. ❌ However, it access unless that server explicitly grants permission.

While SOP enhances security, it also blocks legitimate cross-origin requests, making it difficult to fetch data from external APIs. To solve this, CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) was introduced.

Whenever a browser makes a cross-origin request, it checks the CORS headers in the server’s response. If the response does not include the appropriate headers, the request is blocked.

Here’s a quick breakdown of how it works:

  • : Basic requests without special headers are sent directly, and the browser checks the response.
  • : If the request includes custom headers, credentials, or methods like or , the browser first sends an request. If the server responds correctly, the actual request is sent.

Some key CORS headers include:

  • Access-Control-Allow-Origin: Specifies allowed domains ( means any domain, which can be risky).
  • Access-Control-Allow-Methods: Defines allowed HTTP methods (, etc.).
  • Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Lists allowed custom headers.
  • Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: Determines if cookies and authentication headers can be included.

Security Risks of Poor CORS Configuration

Many developers use just to get things working, but this can create serious security vulnerabilities.

🚨 Misconfigurations That Weaken Security

    • Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * allows website to access your API.
    • This is especially dangerous when combined with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true, which exposes sensitive data.
    • Allowing all methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS) increases the risk of unintended actions.
    • Example: If is allowed, an attacker could delete user data via CSRF attacks.
  3. Weak Preflight Request Handling
    • Some servers improperly respond to preflight requests, allowing unauthorized actions.
    • Example: If Access-Control-Allow-Methods: * is set, attackers can exploit hidden API methods.

Best Practices for Secure CORS Configuration

To enhance security, CORS should be configured with strict rules instead of allowing everything.

✅ Secure CORS Configuration 

add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' 'https://trusted.com';

Instead of *, explicitly list trusted domains.

add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' 'GET, POST';

Only allow methods necessary for your API.

add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' 'Content-Type, Authorization';

Avoid allowing all headers ().

Handle Preflight Requests Securely

add_header 'Access-Control-Max-Age' '3600';

Caches preflight responses to reduce unnecessary requests.

Be Cautious with Credentials

add_header 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials' 'true';

Only use this if absolutely necessary and never with as the origin.

CORS is essential for modern web applications, but a poorly configured CORS policy can expose your API to security risks. Instead of just "fixing" CORS errors, always follow these best practices:

✅ Allow only trusted origins.

✅ Restrict HTTP methods and headers.

✅ Secure preflight request handling.

✅ Avoid using with credentials.

By applying these principles, you can keep your web applications both functional and secure.

items.title: Electromagnetic Shielding: Picking the Perfect Faraday Box
items.author: Mills Lorentzen
items.content:
In an significantly connected world, the necessity for security against electromagnetic interference has never been more crucial. Since devices become more sophisticated, so do the threats they deal with from technologies such as EMPs and broadcast frequency interference. This is when the Faraday container comes into have fun, serving like an essential barrier involving the important electronics along with the outdoors electromagnetic chaos. Regardless of whether this link 're a technical enthusiast wanting to safeguard gadgets or perhaps a company owner tasked along with protecting sensitive tools, choosing the appropriate Faraday box is essential.

With various alternatives available on the market, looking for the perfect Faraday box tailored in order to your particular needs could feel overwhelming. In this guide, we'll explore the main element functions to look with regard to in the reliable Faraday box, ensuring an individual make the best choice. From understanding the elements that provide successful shielding to considering size and potential, our comprehensive tips will help you navigate the choices. Find ready to find out the particular a good Faraday box in addition to how to find the one which presents maximum protection with regard to your electronics.

Key Top features of an Effective Faraday Box When selecting some sort of Faraday box, the shielding effectiveness is definitely one of the particular most critical benefits to consider. Some sort of high-quality Faraday container needs to be designed applying materials that successfully block electromagnetic job areas, including both lower and high-frequency signs. Look for options made from metals such as aluminum or metallic, which have tested efficacy in preventing electromagnetic interference. The box should also supply complete enclosure in order to prevent any penetration of electromagnetic signals, ensuring that your devices are completely protected from EMPs and other kinds of interference.

Another feature is the size and capacity in the Faraday box. The box should be huge enough to support all of your crucial electronics, ensuring that will even bulky things can fit easily without damaging all of them. It is vital to measure the dimensions of your equipment and compare these to the internal proportions from the Faraday container. Additionally, some bins have customizable or stackable options, which in turn can enhance storage capabilities for various forms of equipment.

Durability is yet a key consideration think about a Faraday package. The materials should be robust enough to withstand physical impacts, environmental changes, and continuous use. Look regarding products that present rugged construction, which include reinforced corners and even a secure drawing a line under system. Ensuring of which the box is waterproof or resistant to dirt can be beneficial, especially if you plan to use it outside or in varying climates. A tough Faraday box will provide long-term defense and help protect the integrity of the valuable electronics.

Choosing typically the Right Faraday Box for Your Demands If deciding on a Faraday container, you will need to consider typically the specific electronics or perhaps devices you would like to shield. Different devices may well have varying requirements for electromagnetic safeguarding, making it vital to match typically the box's specifications to your equipment. Take into account the type of defense you will need, such while safeguarding against electromagnetic pulses or daily electronic interference, to be able to ensure the container you decide on provides adequate security.

Another factor in order to consider is the sizing and capacity associated with the Faraday container. Take an products from the devices you plan to maintain in addition to measure their dimensions to find some sort of box that presents ample space with no compromising on safeguarding effectiveness. A well-sized box not simply defends your items but also allows intended for easy organization and access, ensuring an individual can quickly retrieve your essentials when needed.

Finally, assess the substance quality and building of the Faraday box. Resilient elements not just enhance the box's shielding capabilities and also ensure long-lasting security for your devices. Look for features this sort of as reinforced seams, secure closures, plus robust outer shells that can endure wear and rip. By centering on these aspects, you'll always be more likely in order to look for a Faraday field that meets your own needs for successful electromagnetic shielding and even device protection.

Testing in addition to Evaluating Your Faraday Box Once you possess chosen a Faraday box, it is crucial to assure that it performs effectively. Start by screening the box's protecting capability against various electronic devices. For any basic test, create a cell phone inside the box and call it through another phone. In case the call goes via, this means that that the particular Faraday box may well not be properly shielding against electromagnetic interference. Conversely, if the phone inside the box would not ring, it suggests that the shielding is working as planned.

Along with basic testing, check into more sophisticated strategies for evaluating the effectiveness of the Faraday box. Think about using RF (radio frequency) signal scanning devices or field strength meters to get a detailed evaluation. These tools could help you determine the ability regarding the box to dam different frequencies, particularly if you need protection against specific threats such as EMPs or high-frequency alerts. This scientific process can give a person a clearer image of how nicely the meets your own protection needs.

Finally, toughness and construction should also be assessed during your assessment. Inspect the materials used in the box to see any kind of signs of use or damage after initial tests. A dependable Faraday box have to withstand regular employ while maintaining its shielding capabilities. Make sure to evaluate how portable it really is if you prepare to move that frequently and whether or not it can keep up against different environmental conditions with regard to long-term protection.

items.title: Scaling Your Cloudflare D1 Database: From the 10 GB Limit to TBs
items.author: Ahmed Rakan
items.content:
D1 is Cloudflare's managed serverless database built on SQLite, offering a lot of out-of-the-box features that make it highly suitable for startups and even enterprises. However, one limitation of D1 is the 10 GB per database restriction. Cloudflare recommends splitting your data across multiple databases, using a per-tenant or per-entity structure.

From my research, there’s no default implementation for per-tenant databases, but it is possible to implement this using Cloudflare APIs—both for D1 and Workers. You can create databases via the API and bind them to Workers using the Workers REST API.

This design choice is important for my architecture, as I plan to stick with Cloudflare's infrastructure for the next few years. However, the implementation can be time-consuming and complex, though it scales well as the number of users grows.

For those seeking an easier solution that provides more runtime space without exceeding the 10 GB limit, you can scale beyond 10 GB—up to 50 GB, for example. Once you hit around 20 GB, the solution might start to break down, at which point hiring skilled engineers to resolve this becomes a viable option. Cloudflare's support for up to 50,000 databases per Worker offers massive scalability potential.

However, it's crucial to decide on the number of shards in advance. But what exactly are shards?

Sharding involves splitting large datasets into smaller, more manageable chunks, called shards, which are stored across multiple servers. This is especially useful when your database exceeds the 10 GB limit of D1.

For example, if your database grows to 1 TB (1000 GB) over 10 years, you’ll need around 100 separate D1 databases. Instead of storing all data in one, which would compromise performance, you distribute it across multiple shards—each shard is just a D1 database with a unique identifier.

But how do you determine which data goes into which shard? Here are two possible strategies:

This method involves creating a hash of the customer ID and distributing customers across specific shards based on the hash.

  • :
    • Ensures even distribution of data across shards.
  • :
    • The 10 GB limit per database still applies, so adding more shards requires rebalancing. This can be mitigated by linking the shard number or key to the user during registration.
    • We need to determine customer id on registeration which make auto increment Integer primary keys absoulte uuid may be more suitable here assigned to the user on success creation.

This approach assigns specific ranges of users (e.g., every 100 or 1000 users) to their dedicated shard.

  • :
    • Easy to implement and scale as the user base grows.
  • :
    • Requires tracking user counts across shards.
    • Some shards may become overwhelmed while others remain underused, we need to implement balancing.

For simplicity and proof of concept, I will implement hash-based sharding. We’ll start with 3 shards, providing 30 GB of storage, which should suffice for hundreds or thousands of users. By the time we reach 10,000 users, Cloudflare will likely have addressed this issue, or we can migrate to a different database solution.

Note: With this setup, Cloudflare allows up to 5000 shards with R1 DB, but this requires custom scripts and contacting Cloudflare support for rate limiting and other considerations.

Step-by-Step Implementation

Step 1: Create the Databases

First, we’ll create 3 databases:

npx wrangler d1 create SHARD_1
npx wrangler d1 create SHARD_2
npx wrangler d1 create SHARD_3

Step 2: Update with Database Bindings

Next, bind the databases to the Worker:

Step 4: Get database Shard Method setup

This will allow us to rederive the right shard based on the hashing function output

Step 4: Implement Hash Function to Determine the Shard

This is very basic implementation you could go with more complex robust hash function implementation. But the goal of this is simply when we compute hash(customer_id) % num_of_our_shards will always give us a number from 0 to num_of_our_shards - 1

Step 5: Create the Worker to Communicate with Shards

  1. Cloudflare D1 Scalability: While Cloudflare D1 offers scalability, it is inherently limited by SQLite's capabilities.

  2. Per-Tenant Database: A per-tenant database approach is more scalable for enterprises and can be automated, though it introduces increased complexity. This guide highlights how to implement this solution.

  3. Hash-Based Sharding for Startups: Sharding by hash with 10 shards provides an effective solution for startups, offering 100 GB of storage. This setup ensures sufficient room for growth and scalability, giving you peace of mind without worrying about hitting any limit, any time soon, however do monitor and adjust. Or I am sure after you pass 5 GB you can hire brilliant engineers who would design a solution for you in a launch break.

  4. Always consider the pro's and con's of each approach.

items.title: Map vs Set: Modern Alternatives to Objects in JavaScript
items.author: Ayako yk
items.content:
Objects are a fundamental part of JavaScript, allowing us to store and manipulate key-value pairs. However, they come with some limitations. To address these shortcomings, and were introduced in 2015 (ES6). In this post, I'll explain what they are, how they work, and how they differ from traditional objects.

A is a collection of keyed items. What sets it apart from an is that keys can be of any type, while keys in Objects must be of type or .

let map = new Map();

map.set("1", "string 1");
map.set(1, "number 1");
map.set(true, "boolean");

console.log(map.get("1")); // string 1
console.log(map.get(1)); // number 1

let obj = { name: "John" };

map.set(obj, "user");
console.log(map.get(obj)); // user

Some other differences between Maps and Objects, as listed on MDN, are:

The keys in a are ordered by the order of their insertion, while the keys in Objects are not always ordered.

A with user-entered values is safe because those values do not override the existing properties of the itself. In contrast, user-entered values in an can override its existing properties.

A is a collection of unique values without keys. Each value in a is unique --- duplicate values are not allowed.

let set = new Set();

let apple = { fruit: "apple"};
let banana = { fruit: "banana"};
let orange = { fruit: "orange"};

set.add(apple);
set.add(banana);
set.add(orange);
set.add(apple); // Ignored
set.add(banana); // Ignored
set.add(orange); // Ignored

console.log(set); 
// Set(3) { { fruit: "apple" }, { fruit: "banana" }, { fruit: "orange" } }
console.log(set.size);  // 3

If we add arrays with the same values, they are treated as different entries, even if the content is identical. This is because arrays are reference types, and each array is a distinct object in memory.

set.add(["fruit", "apple"]);
set.add(["fruit", "apple"]);
set.add(["fruit", "apple"]);

console.log(set.size);  // 3

In summary, and offer new ways to manage collections of data, providing features like flexible key types and unique value storage. I'll continue learning and writing about objects, including topics like prototypes and classes.

items.title: Stop Wasting Time Writing Code for APIs
items.author: David Brown
items.content:
Let’s be honest: API development shouldn’t be this painful.

Yet, every developer knows the grind. You set up authentication, define endpoints, handle errors, write documentation, secure access, and manually integrate APIs with databases and external services. Oh, and don’t forget about versioning, testing, and debugging when something inevitably breaks.

Why are we still doing this the hard way?

Why Traditional API Development Is Slower Than It Should Be

APIs power everything—from microservices to mobile apps—but building them is often a slow, repetitive process. Here’s why:

  • Boilerplate code never stops. Every project requires authentication, logging, request validation, and error handling. It’s the same code, over and over.
  • Manual integrations waste time. Connecting APIs to databases, third-party tools, or internal systems often involves tedious configuration.
  • Security isn’t plug-and-play. Managing API keys, OAuth, and access control is essential but also a pain to implement consistently.
  • Making changes means worrying about breaking clients, rewriting docs, and managing versioning.

Most of this work isn’t difficult—it’s just time-consuming and unnecessary.

What If APIs Were Easier to Build?

That’s exactly why we built —to automate the redundant parts of API development while giving developers full control where they need it. Instead of spending hours setting up endpoints, Martini lets you focus on building APIs that solve real problems.

APIs That Don’t Trap You in a Box

Rethink How You Build APIs

Developers shouldn’t be stuck doing the same tedious setup work for every API. Martini lets you build faster, scale effortlessly, and stop wasting time on repetitive coding.

So if you’re tired of rewriting the same API logic , maybe it’s time to try something different.
